label: AI is for Heavy Lifting, Humans for the Heart
prompt:
A 16:9 horizontal editorial illustration that explains ONE idea: "AI does the hard mechanical work so humans can focus on the heart of the mission".

Composition (a snag): Pulse stands center-left, both stubby arms raised overhead, legs bent deep, straining with full effort to hoist an enormous iron barbell — the weight comically oversized relative to Pulse's small body. The barbell plates are simple and bold; the whole scene reads as maximum effort, deadpan competence. Pulse is mid-lift: knees bent, body fully committed, not decorating. The right two-thirds of the frame is generous open cream paper — negative space — where the two-line quote lives. Generous negative space overall (≥40% of the canvas); Pulse and barbell occupy the left ~45% of the frame, large and confident.

The mascot is a solid OPAQUE shape in front of the scene — no ground line, table edge, horizon, or prop passes through its body; background lines stop at its silhouette. The barbell is held clearly overhead in both hands with visible separation from the torso.

CHARACTER (locked, keep exactly on the reference model): the recurring mascot — a plump teardrop/blood-drop body (fat and rounded at the bottom, narrowing to a gentle pointed tip at the top) filled solid blood red (#c0392b), one rounded-rectangle screen face centered on the wide lower portion with a cream-white fill and two small dot eyes, blank deadpan (no eyebrows, no mouth), one short antenna with an accent-colored ball tip (orange), small stubby arms and legs in the same blood-red fill. It MUST perform the move — straining to hold the barbell overhead — not decorate. The body is blood red; the screen window is cream/paper-colored; the dot eyes are dark red or navy; the antenna ball uses THIS palette's accent (orange #ff6a1a), even if the reference sheet shows a different hue.

LINE LANGUAGE: draw EVERYTHING — mascot, barbell, quote lettering — in ONE bold, even-weight, softly-rounded outline (clean vinyl-sticker line), not thin scratchy sketch lines.

STYLE: risograph print — grainy halftone texture, slight ink-layer offset, faint paper grain, flat fills, no gradients, no soft shadows.

PALETTE: paper #fbf7ee. Structure ink #1b2a6b (navy) for all linework, forms, and all lettering. Accent #ff6a1a (orange) used sparingly — the antenna ball + one small heart accent only.

LABELS: In the open right half of the image, hand-letter a two-line quote as clean, confident display type directly on the bare cream paper — large enough to read clearly (roughly 1/5 of the image height per line), in the structure-ink navy color (#1b2a6b), NO fill behind it, NO box or underline, NO border:
  Line 1: "AI is for Heavy Lifting,"
  Line 2: "Humans for the Heart!"
A small hand-drawn accent heart in the orange accent color (#ff6a1a) follows the exclamation mark on line 2 — tiny, no larger than a punctuation mark. No other text, no title bar, no logo, no type label anywhere.


Aspect ratio: 16:9.
